import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Analyze income by categorical
features = data.select_dtypes(include=[object]).columns.to_list()
target = 'RainTomorrow'

for feature in features:
    # Corss-tabulation (percentage)
    crosstab = pd.crosstab(data[feature], data[target], normalize='index') * 100

    # Sort by No percentage
    crosstab = crosstab.sort_values('No', ascending=False)

    # Create grouped bar chart
    fig = go.Figure()

    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name='Yes',
        x=crosstab.index,
        y=crosstab['Yes'],
        marker_color='#667eea'
    ))

    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name='No',
        x=crosstab.index,
        y=crosstab['No'],
        marker_color='#f093fb'
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"{target} by {feature.replace("_", " ").title()}",
        xaxis_title=feature.replace("_", " ").title(),
        yaxis_title='Percentage (%)',
        barmode='stack',
        width=1000,
        height=500,
        xaxis={'tickangle': 45}
    )

    fig.show()

    # Print insights
    print(f"
{feature} with higher No rate:")
    print(crosstab.nlargest(5, 'No'))
